description: Chilean national referendum on proposed new constitution drafted by the Constitutional Convention. Rechazo (Reject) won 61.9% vs Apruebo (Approve) 38.1% with 85.8% turnout (mandatory voting reinstated). Tests dramatic reversal from initial enthusiasm to overwhelming rejection.

key_events.description: Constitutional Convention begins work. Controversial proposals on plurinationality and indigenous rights generate media debate.
Convention members' scandals (fake cancer claim by Rojas Vade) erode public trust in the drafting process.
Draft text published. 388 articles seen as too radical; critics highlight abolition of Senate and nationalization provisions.
Rechazo campaign gains momentum across political spectrum. Center-left politicians begin distancing from the text.
Final polls show Rechazo with commanding 20+ point lead. Mandatory voting expected to amplify rejection among disengaged voters.

calibration_notes: Tests dramatic opinion reversal from strong initial support to landslide rejection. The 2019 estallido social created overwhelming demand for a new constitution (78% Apruebo in 2020 entry plebiscite), but the Convention's maximalist proposals alienated the center. Key dynamics: elite fragmentation within the pro side, process legitimacy collapse, mandatory voting bringing in moderates who broke heavily for Rechazo, gap between activist enthusiasm and median voter preferences.
